<<if statement>> <<to switch>> <<control flow>> <<variant>> <<is required>> <<for the>> <<given conditional>> <<structure>>. <<The original>> <<if statement>> <<evaluates the>> <<grade variable>> <<and assigns>> <<a corresponding>> <<gradeValue>>. <<To convert>> <<this into>> <<a switch>> <<statement>> <<while maintaining>> <<optimal efficiency>> <<we must>> <<ensure that>> <<each case>> <<is properly>> <<handled and>> <<that the>> <<default case>> <<is included>> <<to manage>> <<unexpected inputs>>.

<<The switch>> <<statement>> <<in JavaScript>> <<can be used>> <<to replace>> <<the if-else>> <<chain>> <<when the>> <<condition is>> <<based on>> <<equality checks>> <<of a single>> <<variable>>. <<This is>> <<efficient because>> <<it uses>> <<a lookup table>> <<under the>> <<hood for>> <<case matching>>.

<<Here is>> <<the equivalent>> <<switch statement>>:

<<switch (grade)>> <<{>>  
<<case "A":>>  
<<gradeValue = 4.0;>>  
<<break;>>  
<<case "B":>>  
<<gradeValue = 3.0;>>  
<<break;>>  
<<case "C":>>  
<<gradeValue = 2.0;>>  
<<break;>>  
<<case "D":>>  
<<gradeValue = 1.0;>>  
<<break;>>  
<<default:>>  
<<// handle unexpected grades if needed>>  
<<break;>>  
<<}>>.

<<This switch>> <<version is>> <<more efficient>> <<than the>> <<original if-else>> <<chain when>> <<the number>> <<of conditions>> <<is large>> <<because it>> <<avoids multiple>> <<equality checks>> <<and uses>> <<a more optimized>> <<execution path>>. <<The break>> <<statements are>> <<essential to>> <<prevent fall-through>> <<behavior and>> <<ensure that>> <<only the>> <<intended case>> <<is executed>>.
